data.title: Ciena Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 misses by $0.11, revenue of $1.12B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Ciena press release (NYSE:CIEN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 misses by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $1.12B (-0.9% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • Cash and investments at the end of fiscal year 2024 totaled $1.33 billion.
  • Cash flow from operations totaled $349.3 million and $514.5 million for the fiscal fourth quarter and the fiscal year 2024, respectively.
  • Average days' sales outstanding were 89 and 96 for the fiscal fourth quarter and the fiscal year 2024, respectively.
  • Accounts receivable, net balance was $908.6 million.
  • Unbilled contract assets, net balance was $127.9 million.
  • Inventories totaled $820.4 million.
  • Product inventory turns were 2.7 and 2.3 for the fiscal fourth quarter and the fiscal year 2024, respectively.

data.title: Sonic Automotive acquires Audi New Orleans
data.description:
• Sonic Automotive (NYSE:SAH) said it has acquired Audi New Orleans, located in Louisiana.
  • The firm bought Audi New Orleans from the Bohn Brothers Automotive.
  • Audi New Orleans had started operations in June 2009.
  • The company's franchise automotive dealership portfolio now stands at 108 locations nationwide.

More on Sonic Automotive

  • Sonic Automotive raises dividend by 16.7%
  • Sonic Automotive: Online Presence Expansion, Inorganic Growth, Very Undervalued
  • Sonic Automotive's Strategic Response To Digital Car-Buying Trends

data.title: Liquidity Services beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 outlook
data.description:
• Liquidity Services press release (NASDAQ:LQDT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.32 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $106.9M (+33.7% Y/Y) beats by $13.85M.
  • GMV for the fiscal fourth quarter of 2024 was $361.0 million, a 14% increase from $315.6 million in the fourth fiscal quarter of 2023.
  • Non-GAAP Adjusted EBITDA of $14.5 million, up 13%.
  • Cash balances of $155.5 million with zero financial debt.

  • Our Q1-FY25 guidance is as follows:

    $ in millions, except per share data Q1-FY25 Guidance
    GMV $350 to $385
    GAAP Net Income $2.5 to $5.0
    Non-GAAP Adjusted EBITDA $9.5 to $12.5
    GAAP Diluted EPS $0.08 to $0.16
    Non-GAAP Adjusted Diluted EPS $0.18 to $0.26 vs. consensus of $0.22

data.title: PHX Minerals to initiate strategic alternatives process
data.description:
• PHX Minerals (NYSE:PHX) said it will commence a process to evaluate certain strategic alternatives.
  • The alternatives could be involve a possible merger or sale of the firm.
  • The company has retained RBC Capital Markets as its financial advisor to assist in the review process.

More on PHX Minerals 

  • PHX Minerals GAAP EPS of $0.03 in-line
  • WhiteHawk Energy reiterates proposal to acquire PHX Minerals for $4/share
  • Historical earnings data for PHX Minerals

data.title: NIO aims to double sales in 2025 and keep a lid on costs
data.description:
Chinese electric vehicle maker NIO (NYSE:NIO) CEO William Li said the company wants to improve efficiency and cost controls, even as it also looks to spark sales growth that is two years behind schedule. NIO (NIO) has already trimmed its workforce and deferred long-term projects that were not expected to boost overall financial performance within three years.

At an event in Shanghai, Li also noted that NIO (NIO) will start production at its third factory in the second half of next year. The target is for the facility to be able to produce monthly deliveries of 20,000 cars for its Onvo brand by March 2025. NIO's (NIO) new sales goal is to double sales in 2025 from the level seen in 2024.

Li told reporters that NIO (NIO) is evaluating the United States' latest restrictions on semiconductor-related exports to China.

Shares of NIO (NIO) edged up 0.2% in premarket trading on Thursday to $4.68 vs. the 52-week range of $3.61 to $9.57. Short interest on NIO (NIO) stands at 9.6% of the total float. Wall Street and Seeking Alpha analysts both have a consensus Buy rating on the stock.

data.title: Olin increases share buyback authorization to $2B
data.description:
• Olin (NYSE:OLN) said its board has approved a new share buyback program, which increases the authorization to $2 billion.
  • On September 30, the firm had remaining authorization to repurchase ~$0.7 billion of its common stock under its existing stock repurchase plan approved in July 2022.

More on Olin 

  • Olin shares sink as PCC plans new chlor-alkali production at Chemours plant
  • Olin Corporation GAAP EPS of -$0.21 misses by $0.25, revenue of $1.59B beats by $10M
  • Early antidumping ruling is somewhat negative for Olin, Westlake: KeyBanc

data.title: Empire Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.73, revenue of C$7.78B; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Empire press release (OTCPK:EMLAF): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.73.
  • Revenue of C$7.78B (+0.4% Y/Y).
  • Same-store sales, excluding fuel, increased by 1.8%.
  • For the quarter ended November 2, 2024, EBITDA increased to C$600.7 million from C$580.4 million in the prior year mainly as a result of the same factors affecting operating income (excluding the increase in depreciation and amortization of C$13.6 million).
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin increased to 7.7% from 7.4% in the prior year.
  • The effective income tax rate for the quarter ended November 2, 2024, was 25.8% compared to 22.3% in the same quarter last year.
  • The company invested C$149.2 million in capital expenditures for the quarter ended November 2, 2024 (November 4, 2023 – C$134.6 million).
  • Outlook: For fiscal 2025, capital spend is expected to be approximately C$700 million, with approximately half of this investment allocated to renovations and new store expansion, 25% allocated to IT and business development projects and the remainder allocated to central kitchens, logistics, sustainability and e-commerce.
  • The Company is on track with its plan to renovate approximately 20% to 25% of the network between fiscal 2024 and fiscal 2026.
  • During fiscal 2025, the company expects aggregate pre-tax earnings from Other income plus Share of earnings from investments, at equity (both found in the Company's Consolidated Statements of Earnings), to be in the range of C$135 million and C$155 million (2024 - C$140.1 million, excluding the gain of C$90.8 million on the Western Canada Fuel Sale).

data.title: Topgolf Callaway closes on sale of its mobile game subsidiary
data.description:
Topgolf Callaway Brands Corp. (NYSE:MODG) disclosed on Thursday that a wholly owned subsidiary completed the sale of the mobile game World Golf Tour business.

World Golf Tour is an online multiplayer virtual golf game platform founded in 2006 by Chad Nelson and YuChiang Cheng. The primary goal of the platform was to create a high-quality, free online golf game simulation that could compete with console video sports games

The company said the divestiture of World Golf Tour aligns with Topgolf's (MODG) strategic objective of innovating and improving on its core competency of bringing players joy through more ways to play the game of golf in its over 100 outdoor Topgolf venues worldwide.

By divesting its interest in World Golf Tour LLC, Topgolf (MODG) will progress with its objective of streamlining its operations. The company said it plans to concentrate resources on further enhancing the player experience at its venues through innovation in venue-based gaming experiences.

Shares of Topgolf Callaway (MODG) are down 42% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Euronet to acquire ATM assets from Swedbank
data.description:
• Euronet (NASDAQ:EEFT) Thursday reported an agreement with Swedbank to acquire the bank’s ATM assets and provide ATM outsourcing services in Lithuania, Estonia and Latvia.
  • Euronet (EEFT) will acquire the bank’s in-branch and off-branch ATM assets in the three Baltic countries, while ensuring seamless migration to Euronet’s Ren payments processing platform and integration with the bank’s existing systems.
  • The Swedbank ATM network has 1,141 ATMs (373 in Estonia, 362 in Latvia and 406 in Lithuania) and includes cash withdrawal and cash deposit/recycling ATMs.
  • The migration will start in the fall of 2025.
  • EEFT -0.82% premarket to $104.05.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: European NATO members said to weigh 3% defense spending target ahead of Trump's return
data.description:
European members of NATO are discussing raising the military alliance's defense spending target to 3% of GDP from the current 2%, the Financial Times reported, citing four people involved in the preliminary talks. The discussions are said to be partly in anticipation of U.S. president-elect Donald Trump taking office in January.

The talks, which began last week and could face stiff opposition, involve a potential short-term pledge to reach 2.5% and then 3% by 2030. The increased commitments would be brought up at the annual summit in June in the Netherlands.

For now, 23 of the 32 NATO members are expected to reach the existing 2% target this year. But European countries like Belgium, Italy and Spain are failing to meet the commitment.

The discussions to raise the target began amid concerns that current spending levels may not be enough to support Ukraine. In addition, Trump has repeatedly criticized European countries for not meeting the agreed upon target. He recently said he would "absolutely" consider leaving NATO if members didn't "pay their bills."

A commitment to 3% of GDP would be a "good signal to the U.S. and to Trump," a German official told FT. The U.S. spends an estimated 3.4% of its GDP on defense.
